verdicts.reason: The routing slip generator correctly uses existing helpers, handles edge cases for reviewer chains, and implements robust mutual exclusion for drawers, ensuring data integrity and correct output.

The application adheres to its structural constraints regarding CSP and local storage. All user-controlled input is consistently escaped using `esc()` or `escXml()` before being rendered to HTML or included in DOCX XML, mitigating injection risks. No new unmitigated attack surfaces were identified.
The routing slip generator is well-integrated, provides clear affordances, and maintains consistent UX patterns with the rest of the application.
The application provides comprehensive in-app documentation, clear naming, and an explicit AI formatting guide, ensuring high discoverability for both human users and AI agents.
All new features directly address common, recurring pain points in naval correspondence, enhancing efficiency and compliance for real users.
verdicts.evidence: Routing Slip Generator, Template Library, Reply Draft, Address Book, Endorsement Chain, and Letter Status Tracker all solve specific, frequent administrative tasks for naval personnel, saving time and reducing errors.

verdicts.reason: The new features (routing slip, templates, reply draft, status tracker) significantly deepen the tool's utility and reinforce its identity as the indispensable, comprehensive single-file solution for naval correspondence, providing specific, highly valuable workflow enhancements.

[AUTO-DOWNGRADED: LESSONS VETO missing precondition_evidence] The `esc` function uses a regex literal with an embedded double quote (`/"/g, '"'`), which violates the documented `svg-fields` KEEP lesson to use `String.prototype.split(x).join(y)` for quotes to avoid the `test_project.py` brace-balance bug.
verdicts.required_fix: Modify the `esc` function to replace `s.replace(/"/g, '"')` with `String(s).split('"').join('"')` to comply with the documented lesson.
verdicts.evidence: KEEP: `String.prototype.split(x).join(y)` replaces `.replace(/x/g, y)` when `x` is `"` or `'` — dodges the known `test_project.py` brace-balance bug where regex literals with embedded quotes confuse the string-stripper. For XML/HTML escape functions specifically: 5× split/join chain is readable and test-safe. (from `learnings.md` under `[svg-fields]`)
